statement: Structural persistence in far-from-equilibrium systems is explained by at least one of five independent sufficient conditions: (A) BARRIER — kinetic barrier dominance E_a/δ >> 1; (B) THROUGHPUT — active maintenance dominance M/δ > 1; (C) CRITICAL — proximity to critical manifold with divergent ξ or χ; (D) TOPOLOGICAL — discrete invariant Q with threshold-gated failure; (E) NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE — structure requires noise σ_opt > 0, with M/δ undefined or identically 1. Systems may instantiate multiple classes (HYBRID). No single condition is universally necessary.

A_BARRIER.key_distinction: Continuous barrier — finite escape rate for any δ > 0

B_THROUGHPUT.note: M = f(δ) permitted if feedback gain > 0. M/δ may be time-averaged over τ_avg ≤ τ_struct.

C_CRITICAL.condition: system near critical manifold; ξ → ∞ or χ → ∞
C_CRITICAL.scope_guard: Power-law statistics alone are NOT sufficient. Requires demonstrable ξ/χ divergence at a formal critical point.

D_TOPOLOGICAL.condition: discrete invariant Q ∈ ℤ; ΔQ = 0 for all smooth perturbations with δ < E_defect
D_TOPOLOGICAL.failure_timescale: ∞ for δ < E_defect; finite for δ > E_defect
D_TOPOLOGICAL.key_distinction: Discrete threshold — ZERO failure rate below E_defect (not merely small). Contrast Class A: Arrhenius rate is always nonzero.

E_NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE.condition: φ(0) = 0, φ(σ_opt) > 0, φ(∞) = 0; structure requires σ > 0
E_NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE.note: M and δ are NOT independently operationalizable. M/δ ≡ 1 or undefined. Do not assign Class B.

NEUTRAL_PERSISTENCE.description: Persistence via high-dimensional neutral connectivity; no A–E mechanism operative
NEUTRAL_PERSISTENCE.status: UNCLASSIFIED pending test plan; candidate Class F
NEUTRAL_PERSISTENCE.test_plan: Define neutral connectivity measure N_d; test persistence scaling with N_d under drift δ

PATTERN_PERSISTENCE.description: Persistence of dynamical attractor or statistical signature; not state persistence
PATTERN_PERSISTENCE.example: GARCH volatility clustering; intermediate-disturbance species cycling
PATTERN_PERSISTENCE.status: Out of scope for kernel-002; requires PATTERN kernel

EDGE_OF_STABILITY.description: DNN training trajectory — active structural change, not state persistence
EDGE_OF_STABILITY.status: Out of scope — not a far-from-equilibrium state persistence problem

hybrid_rules.dominance_rule: Mechanism with shortest failure timescale dominates failure mode
hybrid_rules.coexisting_hybrid: Mechanisms independently operative; label HYBRID(A+B) etc.; predict asymmetric failure timescales
hybrid_rules.coupled_hybrid: Mechanisms interact; require explicit coupling term f(E_a, M); default to coexisting without coupling term

transition_rules.B_to_C: As M/δ → 1 from above, Class C signatures (divergent Var, 1/f spectrum) should emerge before Class B collapse. See P3.
transition_rules.A_to_B: E_a→0 with M>0: system transitions from passive trapping to active maintenance; HYBRID(A+B) intermediate
transition_rules.any_to_UNCLASSIFIED: If no class condition is formally operationalizable in the domain, assign UNCLASSIFIED and specify test plan

P1_TOPOLOGICAL.claim: Class D failure rate is exactly zero for δ < E_defect; non-zero only above threshold. Contrast: Class A has finite rate for any δ > 0.

P2_NOISE_CONSTRUCTIVE.claim: φ is non-monotonic in σ: φ(0) = 0, φ(σ_opt) > 0, φ(high) < φ(σ_opt)

P3_HYBRID_BC.claim: In COUPLED HYBRID(B↔C), throughput M suppresses early-warning variance: Var_obs/Var_pred = g(M/δ) < 1 for M/δ > 0
P3_HYBRID_BC.fail: Var_obs/Var_pred ≈ 1 for all M/δ (COEXISTING, not COUPLED)

scope_guards: Equilibrium systems excluded (δ ≈ 0; passive stability at ground state)
Class C requires demonstrable ξ/χ divergence; power-law statistics are not sufficient
Mathematical analogs (loss landscape E_a, quorum M) require explicit formal equivalence justification
Neutral persistence is UNCLASSIFIED; do not force into A–E
The taxonomy does not claim to be complete; UNCLASSIFIED is a valid assignment
